fields.content: text string+is used to find a character string regardless of whether its
upper or lower+case.  Text strings may be used

rows.content: to override the system option FIND/CHANGE SEARCH TYPE setting of "C".
A text string is a quoted string that is preceded or followed by the

fields.content: T.+  All alphabetic characters within a text string are treated

rows.content: as if they were upper case and all alphabetic characters in the data
that is being searched are treated as if they were upper case.
If the system option FIND/CHANGE SEARCH TYPE is set to "T", then searches
ignore case by default.  In this event, "T" need not be used in each FIND
command, and "C" can be employed to override the default and cause the

fields.content: find t'ctc'        + find the text "ctc" or "CTC"

rows.content: In the example above, the string "ctc" could be entered in either upper
or lower case, and the FIND command would locate an upper case "CTC", a
lower case "ctc", or any combination: "cTc", "ctC", etcetera.
